_comment: Example: per-image authored descriptions. Pass via `embed_images.py --author-descriptions <this-file>`. Keys are paths relative to `<convert>/embedded_images/`. This file is project-specific — tailor for your docs.
Quantivly_Inc_-_Vendor_-_Non-Device_-_Fu: Mayo ISA Determination Matrix (AI Tools Exhibit). Top-axis 'Footprint category': On-prem products column CIRCLED RED (Devices, Hardware, Software[underlined], Telecom, Products installed into Mayo-owned cloud environments). The NVD column is not selected. Right-side axis 'Data sharing & Internet Connectivity': Contractor access (Individual VPN / Mayo VDI), Data sharing to vendor (SFTP/Signiant, encrypted mail, SharePoint download, Web APIs, Device telemetry, Physical shipment, Manual data entry; plus SaaS/PaaS/IaaS), Internet connections to non-critical Mayo systems, Internet connections to critical Mayo systems (Crown jewels, Class A apps, Site-to-site VPN). **The entire Data sharing & Internet Connectivity block is struck through in red (not applicable to this vendor).** Left-side axis: (top) Limited installations / Limited Mayo network connectivity / Non-critical products; (middle, CIRCLED RED) Division-wide installations / Internal connections to critical Mayo systems / Business-critical products; (bottom, not circled) enterprise-wide. Inner grid 'Data Classification': Public (green); General CBI / Deidentified PHI / Low-level PII (yellow→orange by <500, 500–9999, ≥10 000 records); Regulated CBI / Restricted CBI (yellow→red by <500, ≥500). Each cell lists Probity, Pen test, BitSight, Security audit, and ISA tier (Base, BA, DC, PT, SA). Selected cell (red circle): middle row, On-prem products column — `Probity · ISA: Base + BP + OP`. That is Quantivly's chosen tier.
